description: Completed IPO on NASDAQ at $29.50 per share, raising approximately $1B and converting from a private partnership to a publicly traded corporation. The listing made TPG the last of the major alternative asset managers to go public.
stated_rationale: Access permanent public equity capital, provide liquidity for existing partners, increase brand visibility, and create acquisition currency for inorganic growth. The IPO also enabled broader talent retention through equity compensation.
observable_metric_impact: Raised ~$1B in gross proceeds. Market capitalization of ~$9.4B at IPO. Provided balance sheet firepower for subsequent M&A activity including the Angelo Gordon acquisition.
op_prereq_summary: Required partnership-to-corporation conversion, SEC registration, establishment of dual-class share structure, and financial reporting infrastructure buildout for public company compliance.

description: Acquired Angelo Gordon, a $73B AUM credit and real estate focused alternative investment firm, for approximately $2.7B in a cash-and-stock transaction. The deal represented TPG's largest acquisition and its entry into scaled credit markets.
stated_rationale: Establish a scaled credit platform overnight, diversify fee streams beyond PE-centric model, access Angelo Gordon's direct lending, CLO, and structured credit capabilities, and add durable management fee base with high fee-related earnings margin.
observable_metric_impact: Added ~$73B in AUM (a ~55% increase to TPG's pre-deal base). Credit AUM went from negligible to ~$73B, transforming TPG from a PE-dominant firm into a diversified platform. Fee-related revenue increased significantly in subsequent quarters.
op_prereq_summary: Required IPO-generated acquisition currency (stock component), regulatory approvals, integration of Angelo Gordon's ~600 employees, and consolidation of credit investment operations under the TPG platform.

description: Launched TPG Rise Climate, a dedicated climate investing fund targeting $7B+, focused on decarbonization opportunities across clean energy, enabling technologies, and climate-related infrastructure. The fund operates as a joint venture with leadership from Hank Paulson (former US Treasury Secretary).
stated_rationale: Capitalize on the multi-decade energy transition mega-trend, differentiate TPG's platform with an ESG/impact brand, and access the growing pool of institutional capital mandated for climate-aligned investments. The Rise platform positions TPG uniquely among alt managers as a scaled impact investor.
observable_metric_impact: Rise Climate Fund raised $7.3B at final close (2022), making it one of the largest dedicated climate PE funds globally. Deployed capital into portfolio companies across renewable energy, grid modernization, and sustainable agriculture. Rise Fund franchise (climate + social impact) surpassed $18B in cumulative commitments.
op_prereq_summary: Required partnership with Hank Paulson and senior climate-science advisory board, development of proprietary carbon-impact quantification methodology, and dedicated investment team buildout across sectors.

description: Expanded into real estate with the launch of TPG Real Estate Partners (TREP) and subsequent fundraising rounds, building a dedicated real estate equity and credit platform. By 2024, TPG AG Real Estate (post-Angelo Gordon integration) managed approximately $30B+ across real estate equity and credit strategies.
stated_rationale: Fill a product gap relative to diversified peers (Blackstone, KKR, Carlyle) that generate significant fees from real estate. Leverage Angelo Gordon's existing real estate credit capabilities alongside TPG's equity deal-sourcing network to create an integrated real estate offering.
observable_metric_impact: Real estate AUM scaled from a small base pre-2022 to $30B+ by 2024, primarily through the Angelo Gordon integration. TREP strategies deployed across opportunistic and value-add real estate in the US, Europe, and Asia. Real estate became a meaningful contributor to fee-related earnings.
op_prereq_summary: Required dedicated real estate investment professionals, integration of Angelo Gordon's $20B+ real estate book, and establishment of sector-specialist origination teams across geographies.
